Im actually sad I never went on to chemistry since it looks really fun

I had a friend who worked in a lab that basically worked with a lot of different fly strains(?) not sure if its the correct word but basically the lab was a lab to genetically modify random insects to test it for other purposes.

So one of her main projects was basically she had a tank of normal flies, completely normal, then she had another tank with flies they genetically modified with some algae's DNA
eventually she ended up showing me how it went
so the normal flies were.. normal, to the point the never stop moving, flying, stopping, whatever.
But the flies with the modified DNA overtime (many generations and offsprings until the dna is finally with the desired result)
She showed me the normal ones, with a red looks UV light
She swiped the light on the gen modded ones
the flies look exactly the same
but the two tanks behave completely different
It was the coolest thing I ever seen in terms of bio science
I guess it might seem unethical but if eventually those flies mixed with every fly in the  world
theoretically just having that type of light
weird to think about it going that far
biochem is really smth else tbh
it looks fun and frustrating at the same time
